Same Semitic root h-r-b; semantic drift within the violence/weapon field: Arabic generalized to war, Hebrew specialized to the instrument (sword)
Same Semitic root n-r (fire/light); semantic narrowing in Hebrew from the source (fire) to the instrument that produces light (lamp/candle)

Same Semitic root s-d-q / ts-d-q (truth/righteousness); Arabic ص and Hebrew צ are cognate sibilants. Arabic=one who is truthful (hence friend), Hebrew=one who is righteous; semantic drift within the truthfulness/moral-uprightness field
Same Semitic root b-r-q (lightning/flash); both mean lightning, shared directly inherited from Proto-Semitic, not merely onomatopoeic parallel development
Same Semitic root k-b-r (greatness/magnitude); Arabic=big/great (general magnitude), Hebrew=mighty/powerful (strength aspect of greatness); semantic specialization within the same conceptual field
Both revolve around stretching/extending a surface; Proto-Germanic rek/rec

Arabic qindeel → Latin candela → Old French chandelier → English; documented loanword chain
ب↔F shift + ص↔K/G shift; chain: binsir → finsir → finger; Proto-Germanic fingraz
Latin humus (earth) → homo → humanus; Arabic adam from adeem al-ard (surface of earth); both = creature of earth
Arabic karrar = place to store; French garer = to shelter/store; g↔k consonant equivalence
Old English eam = عمّ, phonetically identical; replaced by Latin avunculus → oncle → uncle; documented in etymology dictionaries

Latin spirare (breathe) parallels Arabic nafas; inspire = to breathe spirit into; n-f-s ↔ s-p-r consonant correspondence
